decision: Regional digital infrastructure inherits cybersecurity exposure from national and European digital systems.
cost: Regional public services, SMEs, and transport-related digital systems remain exposed to identity-based attacks and credential compromise.
trace: Regional services depend on the same identity and access infrastructures used at national and European levels.
notes: Propagation of national digital infrastructure conditions to the regional level.

decision: Regional economic systems increasingly depend on cloud-based platforms and external digital identity providers.
cost: Operational dependency on third-party digital infrastructure and identity systems.
trace: SMEs and public services rely on credential authentication systems and cloud service providers.
notes: Regional reflection of the broader European digital infrastructure model.

decision: Regional resilience requires verifiable digital identity and audit mechanisms for public and private infrastructures.
cost: Need for technical observability, event traceability, and infrastructure verification models.
trace: Technical observatories and demonstrative systems such as EU Digital Reality Check indicate the need for measurable infrastructure resilience.
notes: Regional-level implication of European and national digital resilience initiatives.
